instructions: Check whether a PubTator or PubMed MCP server is configured before invoking the Python script.
Use the correct subcommand (search, metadata, analyze, download) based on the user's request.
Handle rate-limit errors by retrying once after a delay, then recommending an API key or MCP fallback — never retry in a tight loop.
Present search results as a candidate list and wait for the user to choose which papers to triage.
Never auto-triage search results — always require explicit user confirmation before passing a result to triage-paper.
Never assume all PMC articles are downloadable — check open-access status before attempting a PDF download.
Never hardcode API keys; always use environment variables for PUBMED_API_KEY.
